title: Dog Dragging Hind Legs: Causes, Emergency Signs & Triage Guide
meta_desc: If your dog is dragging their hind legs, it is a medical emergency. Learn why immediate veterinary care is vital for paralysis and mobility loss.
content_html:
What is it when a dog drags their hind legs?

Seeing your beloved dog suddenly unable to use their back legs is a frightening experience. This condition, known as hind-limb paralysis or paresis, occurs when the communication between the spinal cord and the rear legs is interrupted. It may manifest as dragging the legs, 'knuckling' (walking on the tops of the paws), or a complete inability to stand or move the lower half of the body.

Is it an emergency?

Yes, this is considered a high-priority medical emergency. Sudden loss of mobility often points to serious neurological or vascular issues, such as Intervertebral Disc Disease (IVDD), spinal trauma, or a 'saddle thrombus' (blood clot). Time is of the essence; the faster a dog receives medical intervention, the higher the likelihood of preserving nerve function and preventing permanent paralysis.

Why a photo or video helps triage

When you contact an emergency clinic, providing a short video of your dog attempting to move can be incredibly helpful for the triage team. A video allows the veterinarian to see the specific nature of the gait, the level of motor control, and whether the issue appears neurological or orthopedic. This helps the hospital prioritize your pet's case and prepare the necessary diagnostic tools, like an MRI or CT scan, before you even arrive.

faqs.question: What are the most common causes of hind leg dragging?
faqs.answer: The most frequent causes include Intervertebral Disc Disease (IVDD), spinal cord injuries, fibrocartilaginous embolism (FCE), and in older dogs, degenerative myelopathy.

faqs.question: Can I wait until morning to see my regular vet?
faqs.answer: No. If your dog is unable to walk or dragging their legs, they should be seen by an emergency veterinarian immediately to prevent permanent damage.

faqs.question: Should I give my dog pain medication at home?
faqs.answer: Do not administer any human or leftover pet medications, as these can interfere with emergency treatments or be toxic. Keep your dog calm and confined until you reach the vet.
